reasoning: The Federal Reserve explicitly ended Quantitative Tightening (QT) on December 1, 2025, and has initiated reserve management purchases of short-term Treasury bills (often referred to as 'technical QE') to alleviate year-end funding pressures. This marks a structural pivot from a shrinking balance sheet to an expanding one. The FRED data for 2025-12-17 already exhibits a $15.2 billion increase to 4.204 million. With the Fed actively purchasing T-bills to address liquidity, the value of SOMA Treasury holdings is heavily favored to grow on a week-over-week basis over the near term. The probability of an increase by the first resolution date (2025-12-28) is highly elevated. At the same time, maintaining strict compliance with the system instructions ('uncertainty should increase with forecast horizon: probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5'), the estimates for 2026-01-20 and 2026-03-21 are progressively pulled closer to 0.5. This accurately reflects the system constraints, while also accounting for the macroeconomic reality that longer horizons leave more room for unforeseen policy shifts, such as early tapers of the newly implemented T-bill purchases if inflation or funding dynamics change.
